title: The Desert of Forty Days
subtitle: Christ led by the Spirit into the wilderness, fasting and tempted, that He might teach us to conquer

stillness: Be still, and withdraw with Him.
If you cannot flee the world in body, flee it in mind.
Behold, I have gone far off in flight, and have remained in solitude.
Make of your own breast a desert, and there keep silence before God.
gospel: After the Lord Jesus was baptized,
He returned from the Jordan full of the Holy Spirit.
And at once, of His own will, He was led into the desert,
to be tempted by the devil.
And there He fasted forty days and forty nights,
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold the Lord Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, turning from the Jordan toward the waste places.
See Him climb a mountain of wondrous height, called Quarantena, between Jericho and Jerusalem,
the very road where the man going down fell among robbers — the place men call Domyn, which sounds as 'blood', for the blood the robbers spilled there.
There, where the first man was figuratively overcome, behold the Son of God, clothed in flesh, come to overcome the devil in very deed.
Behold Him alone among the wild and the bare rocks, fleeing not only the crowds of demons but the company of evil men.
Watch Him stand through forty days and forty nights, tasting nothing — guarding Himself against the enemy who never ceases to assail us.
Mark well: it is added that He fasted by night also, lest you think He ate in the dark.
consider: He went into the desert not unwilling, but with the will to do battle, and to be tempted.
Learn from this: he who enters the desert of penance is the more fiercely tempted by the enemy.
Son, drawing near to the service of God, stand in justice and in fear, and prepare your soul for temptation.
After the bath of regeneration, after the labor of fasting and the holy purpose of life — at once the tempter draws near, and rises against us more sharply.
He envies those who press toward what is better; he lies in wait for them; the good he tempts more than the wicked.
Those he feels he holds by quiet right, he does not trouble to tempt. You are most assailed when you do not know you are assailed.
He did penance, not because He needed it, but to move us to penance and to show it to us by His own example.
Let your penance be pure, that it may please God; let it be harsh, that it may tame the flesh; let it be discreet, that it go not too far.
He fasted to give us, against temptation, the example of fasting: for to him who would conquer the wars of temptation, sobriety is needful.
Fasting is the greatest shield against the devil. By gluttony of the belly Adam was cast from paradise; the flood was deserved by it, and fire fell upon Sodom.
We who are baptized, members of Christ, who have put on Christ and are buried with Him in His death — we must crucify our flesh with its desires.
Reckon yourself dead upon the earth, mortifying in the spirit the deeds of the flesh.
prayer: Lord Jesus, You went into the desert not for Your own need, but for ours.
Lead me by Your Holy Spirit, and not by any feigning of the evil one,
into the desert of my own heart and of quiet contemplation,
that, resting from the noise of the world, I may better wait upon God.
Teach me to crucify my flesh with its desires,
and, leaving the world behind in my mind,
to hunger for the eternal joys alone, as for the manna of the desert.
Prepare my soul for temptation, and gird me, in the bright days and the dark nights alike,
against the enemy who never ceases to assail me.

latinJewel.latin: Nos ergo, exemplo Christi et Joannis, agitemur a Spiritu Sancto, non per hypocrisim a maligno, in desertum loci et solitudinis, vel saltem in desertum nostri pectoris et contemplationis, ut quieti a strepitu mundi, melius Deo vacemus.
latinJewel.english: Let us then, after the example of Christ and of John, be driven by the Holy Spirit — and not, through hypocrisy, by the evil one — into the desert of place and solitude, or at the least into the desert of our own breast and of contemplation, that, resting from the noise of the world, we may better wait upon God.
